executive_summary: NVIDIA delivered FY2026 revenue of roughly $216B (+65% YoY), with net income near $120B and operating income above $130B as Data Center AI infrastructure demand absorbed nearly the entire revenue mix. Gross margin compressed to about 71% GAAP versus roughly 75% in FY2025, reflecting product mix, ramp costs, and inventory/valuation dynamics, while operating margin remained above 60%. Cash generation scaled with revenue: operating cash flow exceeded $100B and free cash flow approached $97B. Export controls and China revenue assumptions are now an explicit swing factor in forward quarters.

trend_narratives.net_income: Net income growth tracked revenue as operating leverage remained extreme; below-the-line items (other income, securities gains/losses, and tax) add volatility quarter to quarter even when core demand is strong.
trend_narratives.net_margin: Net margin was broadly stable YoY in percentage terms after FY2026’s scale-up; incremental margin pressure from mix or export-related revenue gaps would be the main monitorable.
trend_narratives.fcf: Free cash flow rose roughly in line with profitability while capital intensity increased; the company remains FCF-rich versus semiconductors peers, supporting buybacks and strategic investments.

investment_thesis_short: The setup is a dominant AI compute franchise (CUDA/software stack plus accelerators) monetizing a concentrated hyperscaler capex cycle—balanced by geopolitical/export risk, customer concentration, and the inevitable competition from custom silicon and second-source GPUs.
